name: Chilled Chilied Chicken Wings
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the chili powder, cumin, hot paprika, oregano, salt, and pepper together with the boiling water in a bowl. Stir to make a chili paste.
2. Add ketchup and hot pepper sauce; mix well. Set aside to cool slightly.
3. Place eggs in a large bowl. Stir in the chili paste. Mix well.
4. Cut tapered joint from wings and reserve for stock or another use. Place trimmed wings in the spice egg mixture. Toss thoroughly.
5. Marinate in refrigerator for several hours; longer marination results in spicier wings.
6. Heat oil in a heavy skillet to 375°F (190°C). Fry wings in batches for about 8 minutes, flipping once during cooking. Ensure pan is not crowded.
7. Drain on paper towels and serve hot or chilled.
